items.title: More people are using this 'secret deal' to get an EV. Here's what to know

children.content: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering purchasing an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

items.title: Fuel crisis driving some farmers to consider abandoning harvests

children.content: Tasmanian farmers are considering not harvesting crops like broccoli and cauliflower because increasing freight costs are proving to be so debilitating.

image.altText: a tractor is parked next to a diesel fuel pump

items.title: Activist ship deliberately collides with trawler as 'krill wars' escalate

children.content: An environmental activist group’s ship has deliberately collided with a large fishing trawler in Antarctic waters. The activists say it was a "gentle nudge" but the trawler's owner says a line has been crossed.

image.altText: View from the deck of a ship as it crashes into another ship.

items.title: Arnhem Land rock art raises questions about mainland Tasmanian tigers

articles.title: The 20,000 sailors who have become sitting ducks in the Gulf

articles.title: How the Iran war is pushing US ally the Philippines into economic crisis

children.content: Countries heavily reliant on imported oil, like the Philippines in Southeast Asia, are most vulnerable to the unfolding energy crisis caused by the US-Israel war with Iran.

items.title: How do you clean a toilet brush and when should you change it?

items.title: Cyclone Narelle's destructive path across Australia, in pictures and video

children.content: From the very first news reports about Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle, it was clear this wasn't just another storm. This is the story of how the cyclone impacted the entire span of northern Australia and how communities came together to pick up the pieces.

items.title: I find myself weeping again for Lebanon
